DNB (Diplomate-National Board) Program

DNB (Diplomate-National Board) Program

DNB (Diplomate-National Board) Program

The DNB program is a prestigious postgraduate qualification by the National Board of Examinations (NBE) in India, specializing in ophthalmology

Course Overview

The DNB program is a prestigious postgraduate medical qualification offered by the National Board of Examinations (NBE) in India. It is designed for medical professionals aiming to specialize in various fields, including ophthalmology. The detailed aspects of the course as offered by the eye hospital are given below.

Eligibility Criteria

Required Qualifications: Candidates must have completed either an MBBS (Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery) or a DO (Diploma in Ophthalmology) to be eligible for this course.

Purpose: The eligibility criteria ensure that only qualified medical graduates with a foundational understanding of medicine or ophthalmology are admitted into the program, preparing them for advanced specialization.

Number of Seats Available

Primary Seats: 2 seats are available. These seats are typically for candidates who have completed their MBBS and wish to pursue the DNB course directly.

Secondary Seats: 2 seats are available. These seats are generally reserved for candidates who have already completed a diploma in ophthalmology (DO) and are looking to further their education by obtaining a DNB.

NBEMS Diploma Seats: 4 seats are available. These seats are designated for candidates who have completed their MBBS and are pursuing a further specialized diploma through the National Board of Examinations.

Course Commencement Date

Determination: The exact start date of the course is determined by the National Board, which is responsible for standardizing medical education across various institutions.

Timing: This ensures that the commencement aligns with the national academic calendar and standardized examination schedules.

Medium of Instruction

Language: English, The use of English as the medium of instruction aligns with international standards and ensures that students are prepared for global medical practices and research.

Nodal Officer

Assigned Officer: Murukan V

Role: The nodal officer is the primary point of contact for students and administrative authorities. They are responsible for overseeing the course’s administration, addressing student concerns, and ensuring compliance with NBE guidelines.

Started Year

Inception: 2005

History: The course has been running since 2005, reflecting the institution’s long-standing commitment to providing advanced ophthalmology education. Over the years, it has likely evolved to incorporate new medical practices, technologies, and teaching methodologies.
